title: Delete dead (and broken) LoggingProvider / createLoggingProvider
description: api/src/services/llmProviders.ts:1091-1183 defines LoggingProvider and createLoggingProvider. Grep over the whole repo shows zero importers — only the definitions exist. Worse, the code is broken in three ways that would mislead anyone who tries to use it: (1) line 1147 calls this._provider.stream(...) but every provider implements chatStream, not stream; (2) lines 1125/1152-1154 read usage.prompt_tokens/completion_tokens but providers return { inputTokens, outputTokens }, so the token log would always print '?'; (3) lines 1158-1159 read chunk.content/chunk.thinking but stream chunks carry { type, text }. It is a stale wrapper from a previous provider-result shape.
sketch: Delete lines 1091-1183 (the '── Logging Wrapper ──' section and createLoggingProvider). No call sites to update; api tsc + tests stay green.
impact: 93 lines removed; eliminates a trap that contradicts the live provider interface
guidance: Sketch is correct as-is. Delete lines 1091-1183 inclusive (section comment '── Logging Wrapper ──' through the closing brace of createLoggingProvider); line 1090 closes createProvider so the file ends cleanly. No call sites, no test references — api/tests/handoff.test.ts:9 only auto-mocks the module and does not touch createLoggingProvider. Do not attempt to 'fix' the wrapper instead of deleting it; per-provider logging already exists inline (e.g., the chatStream console.log at line 584).

title: Delete dead swarmService.ts (CommonJS relic of the file-based agent store)
description: api/src/services/swarmService.ts (120 lines) has zero importers anywhere in the repo. It is also internally inconsistent with the codebase: it uses require()/__dirname/module.exports (lines 1-7, 114-121) inside a TypeScript ESM package — importing it would throw 'require is not defined' at runtime — and it persists agents as JSON files under api/src/data/agents/, a storage model the rest of the code replaced with Postgres (services/database.ts). Functions like executeWorkflow and assignTaskToAgent duplicate (a stale version of) AgentManager/workflow-engine behavior, which is actively confusing to anyone grepping for 'workflow' or 'assign task'.
sketch: git rm api/src/services/swarmService.ts. Nothing imports it; the swarm-config.json / agents/ dirs it manages are not referenced elsewhere.
impact: 120 lines / 1 whole file removed; one fewer false hit when searching for workflow/task-assignment logic
guidance: Sketch is correct as-is: git rm api/src/services/swarmService.ts, then run api tsc + tests as a formality. Trivial nit: the file is 121 lines, not 120. No traps found — no dynamic import, no test fixture, no script references the file or its data paths.

title: Collapse 7 copy-pasted runner-service cases in createProvider into a lookup table shared with ExecutionManager
description: llmProviders.ts:1007-1081 has seven near-identical switch cases (claude-paid, opencode, aider, openclaw, hermes, codex, claudecode) that each build `new VLLMProvider(process.env.X_SERVICE_URL || 'http://x-service:8000', config.model, readSecret('CODER_API_KEY'), config.agentId || null, config.ownerId || null, config.permissions || null, config.llmConfig || null)` — only the env var and default URL differ (claude-paid additionally omits llmConfig and defaults the model). The exact same envvar→default-URL mapping is independently re-declared in executionManager.ts:41-59 (DEFAULT_URLS + URL_ENV_VARS). Adding a new CLI runner today requires touching 3 parallel lists.
impact: ~80 lines down to ~25 across both files; one canonical place to register a runner service
guidance: Three behavior-preservation traps: (1) claude-paid (llmProviders.ts:1007-1015) differs in THREE ways, not two — it defaults the model to 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514', it does NOT forward config.llmConfig (always null even when set), and it shares CLAUDECODE_SERVICE_URL with claudecode. It must stay its own case; folding it into the CLI_RUNNERS set would change what the claudecode runner receives. (2) runnerServiceUrl() must read process.env at call time inside the function body — do not resolve URLs at module load (terminal.ts:38-45 does that; don't copy its pattern into the registry), since createProvider currently re-reads env on every call. (3) ExecutionManager's make() must preserve override precedence: opts?.baseUrl || runnerServiceUrl(type). Additional notes: the registry must include 'sandbox' (used only by ExecutionManager; exclude it and claude-paid from the CLI_RUNNERS set in createProvider); derive ProviderType as keyof typeof RUNNER_SERVICES to eliminate the third parallel list (the hand-written union at executionManager.ts:14 and the literal checks in _normalizeProviderType at :16-19); api/src/routes/terminal.ts:38-45 and api/src/index.ts:108 duplicate the same mapping and can be folded in as a follow-up, but terminal.ts currently resolves env at import time, so converting it changes env-read timing (benign in Docker, but call it out).

title: Deduplicate the three OAuth login flows in middleware/auth.ts
description: Three copies of the same machinery: (1) resolveGoogleRedirectUri (266-274), resolveMicrosoftRedirectUri (412-419) and resolveGitHubRedirectUri (562-570) are byte-identical functions — only the comments differ. (2) The find-or-create-user block is copy-pasted 3x: Google 357-372, Microsoft 504-517, GitHub 665-679 (getUserByXId → link existing by email → else countUsers/first-user-admin → createXUser → provisionNewUser). (3) The jwt.sign + res.json login payload appears 5x with the same fields: /login 147-161, /impersonate 213-231, Google 374-389, Microsoft 519-534, GitHub 681-696. Any change to the login response (e.g. adding a field like tutorialCompletedAt was) must be repeated 5 times.
impact: ~120 lines removed from a 910-line file; login-payload changes become one edit instead of five
guidance: Behavior-preservation corrections to the sketch: (1) Do NOT route /impersonate through sendLoginResponse as sketched — its response (lines 224-231) deliberately omits termsAcceptedAt/tutorialCompletedAt and its JWT adds impersonatedBy; the sketch's common '...terms/tutorial fields' body would add two fields to that wire response. Either leave /impersonate bespoke (recommended — it is 1 of 5 and genuinely different) or gate terms/tutorial behind an option. That also removes the impersonatedBy conditional-spread from the helper entirely. (2) avatarUrl key presence differs: /login (153-161) has no avatarUrl key; the OAuth callbacks include `avatarUrl: user.avatar_url || providerAvatarUrl` even when the value is null (Microsoft/GitHub avatars can be null). Passing extra.avatarUrl=undefined for /login works because res.json/JSON.stringify drops undefined-valued keys, but do not coerce undefined to null or the /login payload gains "avatarUrl":null. (3) In findOrCreateOAuthUser, rename the `email` param — GitHub passes a computed username (profile email OR `${login}@users.noreply.github.com`, lines 656-659), not an email. Call it loginUsername. (4) Preserve exact ordering inside the helper: provider-id lookup → getUserByUsername → linkXId then getUserById refetch → countUsers → role 'admin'/'advanced' (it is 'advanced', not 'basic') → createXUser → provisionNewUser(user.id).catch with the identical 'Provisioning error:' log. (5) When merging the three resolve*RedirectUri functions, consolidate the three comments — each documents its provider's frontend callback route vs plugin dispatcher path; that information should survive in the shared helper's comment. (6) No test coverage pins these payloads (no auth-route tests in api/src/services/__tests__), so verify by diff inspection, not by the green suite.

title: Replace ExecutionManager's 7 parallel provider fields with one Map
description: executionManager.ts declares the same 7 runner types in six parallel places: class fields (62-68), constructor assignments (79-85), ExecutionManagerOptions keys (25-31), the _normalizeProviderType equality chain (16-19), the _getProvider switch (103-113), the destroyAll list (194-205), and the closeCliTerminalSessions array (220-228). Nothing outside the class accesses the named fields (verified by grep), so the per-type fields are pure boilerplate. Adding a runner type means 6 coordinated edits.
impact: ~50 lines removed; new runner type = 1 array entry instead of 6 edits
guidance: Three corrections to the sketch, all behavior-preservation: (1) Do NOT use `this.providers.get(t)!` in _getProvider — the current switch has `default: return this.sandbox` (executionManager.ts:111), a runtime fallback for any value not matching a case. Although unreachable today (every call site passes a value from _normalizeProviderType or from _agentProviders, which only stores normalized values), `get(t)!` would turn that defensive arm into a silent undefined. Use `return this.providers.get(type) ?? this.providers.get('sandbox')!;`. (2) `PROVIDER_TYPES.includes(t)` won't typecheck as written: the argument is `string | undefined | null` and PROVIDER_TYPES is a readonly literal tuple; write `(PROVIDER_TYPES as readonly string[]).includes(t as string) ? (t as ProviderType) : 'sandbox'`. (3) closeCliTerminalSessions (220-240) lists providers in a different order (claudecode, codex, opencode, aider, openclaw, hermes) than PROVIDER_TYPES; since Promise.allSettled fires all calls concurrently and the function returns true if ANY fulfilled true, runtime behavior is identical under Map iteration order — the only observable difference is which provider's rejection the console.warn at 235-237 cites when multiple reject (log-only, acceptable). Keep the docstring at 211-219 explaining why sandbox is excluded. Two optional extensions: derive ExecutionManagerOptions via a mapped type `{ [K in ProviderType as `${K}Options`]?: RunnerOpts }` to kill that parallel list too, and note DEFAULT_URLS/URL_ENV_VARS (41-59) are mechanically derivable (`http://${t}-service:8000`, `${t.toUpperCase()}_SERVICE_URL` — verified true for all 7) so they can collapse into the `make` helper as well, taking the repetition count from 10 sites to 1.

title: Extract one OpenAI-compatible stream-consumption helper used by OpenAI, VLLM and Mistral providers
description: The chunk-consumption loop "for await chunk → abort check → yield {type:'text'} from delta.content → yield {type:'thinking'} from delta.reasoning_content → capture finish_reason → on chunk.usage yield {type:'done', finishReason, usage}" is written three times nearly verbatim: OpenAIProvider._chatCompletionStream (620-647), VLLMProvider.chatStream (833-874), MistralProvider.chatStream (944-970). VLLM adds only cost_usd and runner_session_id forwarding. MistralProvider as a whole (888-981) is essentially VLLMProvider minus custom headers — both wrap an OpenAI client pointed at a different baseURL with the same chat() body and the same usage mapping.
impact: ~90 duplicated lines collapse to one ~20-line generator; stream-protocol fixes (e.g. a new delta field) become one edit instead of three
guidance: Scope to the stream-consumption helper ONLY; drop or defer the optional "fold MistralProvider into an OpenAICompatProvider base" — ping() differs entirely (VLLM: raw fetch ${baseUrl}/v1/models with 5s timeout, 877-885; Mistral: client.models.list(), 973-980), VLLM's constructor carries X-Agent-Id/X-Owner-Id/X-Agent-Permissions/X-LLM-Config header machinery (753-770), chat() differs on cost_usd and X-Task-Id, and the factory reuses VLLMProvider for claude-paid. Traps for the helper: (1) VLLM's runnerSessionId must stay at the TOP LEVEL of the done event while costUsd goes INSIDE usage (864-872) — the sketch's top-level ...mapUsage(chunk) spread is correct, but VLLM's mapper must return { usage, runnerSessionId? }, not fold both into usage; (2) fix the sketch's signature/call mismatch — mapUsage is typed (u: any) but called with the whole chunk; VLLM needs chunk.usage.cost_usd and chunk.usage.runner_session_id (both on chunk.usage), so pass chunk.usage consistently; (3) do NOT absorb _responsesChatStream (650-695), it consumes a different event protocol; (4) keep the done-yield inside the for-await loop as sketched; (5) note the one deliberate normalization: OpenAI's chunk.choices[0] (623) becomes choices?.[0] — safe in practice (OpenAI sends choices: [] on the usage-only chunk) but call it out in the commit; (6) preserve the exact abort error message 'Agent stopped by user'.

title: Deduplicate OllamaProvider chat/chatStream request building and 404-auto-pull retry
description: OllamaProvider.chat (153-204) and chatStream (206-243) duplicate two blocks verbatim: the request body construction including the num_ctx if/else (154-170 vs 207-222, differing only in stream:true/false), and the fetch → if 404 → _pullModel() → identical second fetch → if !ok throw sequence (172-191 vs 224-243). Six copies of the same fetch options literal exist in this one class.
impact: ~50 lines removed; the 404-pull recovery behavior is stated once
guidance: 1) CRITICAL: do NOT use `options.contextLength ?? 8192` as the sketch suggests — use `options.contextLength || 8192`. The original `if (options.contextLength)` is a truthiness check, and contextLength === 0 is a real value in this codebase (api/src/services/agentManager/chat.ts:984, chat.ts:1054, crud.ts:41, index.ts:270/288 all default it to 0). With `??`, Ollama would receive num_ctx: 0 instead of 8192 — a runtime behavior change. 2) Keep the helper call awaited inside the chatStream generator body so the request still fires lazily on first next(), not at generator creation. 3) Preserve the `options.signal || null` normalization (undefined -> null) when passing the signal to ollamaFetchWithRetry. 4) Keep JSON.stringify(body) inside the doFetch closure so both attempts stringify per-call as today (body is not mutated between attempts, so this is belt-and-braces). 5) Do not fold the _pullModel fetch (lines 132-136) into the shared helper — it targets /api/pull with plain fetch and no retry wrapper; only the four /v1/chat/completions fetches are true duplicates.

title: socketHandler: merge the twin task-execute handlers and flatten the voice-management switch
description: Two patterns bloat the 567-line connection callback: (1) REQ_TASK_EXECUTE (300-320) and REQ_TASK_EXECUTE_ALL (323-343) are line-for-line identical (lookup agent → STREAM_START → call manager with chunk-forwarding callback → STREAM_END/STREAM_ERROR) except for the manager method called; the voice REQ_VOICE_DELEGATE (346-403) and REQ_VOICE_ASK (406-452) repeat the same find-target-by-name → not-found-emit → streamStart/sendMessage/streamEnd skeleton. (2) The REQ_VOICE_MANAGEMENT switch (478-580) repeats `const target = findAgent(args.agent_name); if (!target) { result = `Agent "..." not found`; break; }` in 6 of its 11 cases.
impact: ~70 lines removed; each voice function reads as a 3-5 line entry instead of a case buried in a 100-line switch
guidance: Corrections and traps: (a) The sketch's table ctx {target, args} is insufficient — list_agents, get_available_agent, and clear_all_* need userAgents and agentId (self-exclusion), and most cases need agentManager/ws; define the table inside the REQ_VOICE_MANAGEMENT handler so entries close over them (per-event allocation is negligible) rather than widening ctx. (b) Not-found must be emitted as { error: null, result: 'Agent "X" not found' } — never throw from the dispatcher; a throw would route to the catch at socketHandler.ts:583 and flip the frontend (VoiceSessionContext.tsx:419-423) into its error path, changing what the Realtime LLM receives. (c) Dispatcher must `result = await entry.run(ctx)` because cases mix sync and async (clear_context, list_projects, clear_all_chats await). (d) Keep all result strings, per-case console.log lines, and the default `Unknown management function: ${functionName}` message byte-identical — the voice LLM consumes them. (e) streamTaskToSocket must use socket.emit (this socket only) and keep STREAM_START inside the try; keep per-handler destructuring and the canAccessAgent/taskId guards outside the helper. (f) Do NOT extend the merge to REQ_VOICE_DELEGATE/REQ_VOICE_ASK despite the description calling them "the same skeleton": ASK has a busy-check (423), extra sendMessage args (441-442), and no ws.thinking in its chunk callback; DELEGATE calls addTask (375) and uses a different prefix; both use the broadcast ws.streamStart/streamChunk/streamEnd emitter, not socket.emit. Fusing them would change behavior or require flag soup — leave them as-is, as the sketch already does.

title: Split board/project authorization helpers out of middleware/auth.ts
description: middleware/auth.ts is a 910-line god file holding three unrelated concerns: (a) login/OAuth/terms HTTP routes (116-738), (b) the authenticateToken/requireRole middleware (740-764), and (c) the IDOR resource-authorization layer — Permission, checkBoardAccess, authorizeBoardAccess, checkProjectAccess, authorizeProjectAccess (766-908). The authz layer has nothing to do with login routes; it is imported by routes/{boards,projects,tasks,agents}.ts and tested independently (services/__tests__/authorization.test.ts, projectTenantIsolation.test.ts). Finding checkBoardAccess today means scrolling past 700 lines of OAuth plumbing.
sketch: New api/src/middleware/authz.ts containing lines 766-908 verbatim (Permission, PERMISSION_LEVELS, BoardAccessResult, checkBoardAccess, authorizeBoardAccess, ProjectAccessResult, checkProjectAccess, authorizeProjectAccess) plus its database.js imports. Update the 7 importing files (routes/tasks.ts, routes/agents.ts, routes/projects.ts, routes/boards.ts, the two tests, and re-export from auth.ts temporarily if preferred). auth.ts drops to ~740 lines (~600 after the OAuth dedup proposal).
impact: auth.ts shrinks ~145 lines; authorization logic becomes a discoverable, separately-testable module
guidance: Corrections and traps: (1) TEST MOCK TRAP — projectTenantIsolation.test.ts:60-66 uses node:test mock.module('../../middleware/auth.js') to stub requireRole + checkBoardAccess + checkProjectAccess before dynamically importing routes/projects.js and routes/tasks.js. If route files repoint their check* imports to authz.js, this test MUST add a parallel mock.module('../../middleware/authz.js', { namedExports: { checkBoardAccess, checkProjectAccess } }) while keeping requireRole in the auth.js mock — otherwise routes bind the real implementations (failure is loud, 403-vs-404 assertion mismatch, but fix it in the same commit). (2) authorization.test.ts:67 imports check* from '../../middleware/auth.js'; repoint to authz.js (the no-op OAuth stubs at its lines 41-55 then become removable, but leaving them is harmless). (3) Prefer a hard move + updating all 6 importers over a permanent re-export from auth.ts: node:test mock.module keys on the resolved module specifier, so two paths exporting the same symbols invites future mock drift. (4) After the move, drop getBoardById/getBoardShare/getProjectById/hasProjectBoardAccess from auth.ts's import at line 10 — they are unused outside 766-908. (5) Move authorizeBoardAccess (835-851) and authorizeProjectAccess (892-908) VERBATIM even though they are exported-but-never-imported anywhere in the repo (vestigial — routes call the check* functions directly); deleting them is a defensible separate follow-up, not part of this behavior-preserving move. (6) Importer count is 6 (4 route files + 2 tests), not 7. (7) New module needs the NodeNext-style '.js' extension in imports ('../middleware/authz.js') to match the codebase's ESM convention.

title: Table-drive the 13 MCP handler mounts in index.ts
description: index.ts:220-268 repeats the same two-line pattern 13 times: `const xMcpHandler = createXMcpHandler(); app.all('/api/x/mcp', authenticateToken, (req, res) => xMcpHandler(req, res));` (onedrive, gmail, outlook, gdrive, slack, jira, wordpress, github, s3, code-index, gandi-dns, auto-learn, browser, swarm-api). The `(req, res) => handler(req, res)` lambda is also a no-op wrapper — the handler could be passed directly. Only gmail/outlook (exec bridge) and code-index/gandi-dns/swarm-api (constructor args) differ, and only in how the handler is created.
impact: ~48 lines down to ~20; adding an MCP endpoint becomes one array entry
guidance: Corrections/notes for the implementer: (1) It is 14 authenticateToken mounts, not 13 (onedrive, gmail, outlook, gdrive, slack, jira, wordpress, github, s3, code-index, gandi-dns, auto-learn, browser, swarm-api internal), plus the separate authenticateApiKey /api/swarm/mcp mount which must stay outside the table. (2) Preserve the existing registration order in the array (paths are disjoint so it is not behavior-critical, but it is free to keep). (3) Keep the two separate createSwarmApiMcpHandler(agentManager) instances (index.ts:267 internal-JWT vs :271 external-API-key) — do not collapse them into one shared instance even though it would likely be safe. (4) Carry over the explanatory comments: the runner-bridge rationale at index.ts:223-225 (attach it to the shared execBridge const) and the internal-vs-external swarm comments at :266 and :270. (5) Type the table as Array<[string, express RequestHandler-compatible fn]> or use `any` as sketched — three factories (jira, wordpress, outlook) annotate (req: any, res: any), so a strict RequestHandler tuple may need the loose typing the sketch already uses.

title: Use authenticateToken middleware in /accept-terms and /complete-tutorial instead of hand-rolled JWT parsing
description: auth.ts:705-720 (/accept-terms) and 723-738 (/complete-tutorial) each re-implement the auth-header split + jwt.verify + JsonWebTokenError/TokenExpiredError handling that authenticateToken (741-753) already provides — the same try/catch body duplicated twice, differing only in which DB function is called and which field is echoed back. (/verify at 169-198 also parses manually, but it legitimately differs: it fetches fresh user data and reports impersonation, so leave it.)
impact: ~16 lines removed; one JWT-validation path instead of three in the same file
guidance: Sketch is correct as-is. Two notes: (1) the reviewer's caveat is resolved — frontend matches on status/generic toast, never on the 'No token provided' vs 'Access denied' message text, so the wording change is safe; the only user-visible effect is toast text in the degenerate no-token-in-localStorage case. (2) When applying, drop the JsonWebTokenError/TokenExpiredError branch from the handler catch entirely (as the sketch does) — after the middleware runs, jwt errors can no longer reach the handler, so keeping that branch would be dead code. Keep the per-route console.error prefixes ('Accept terms error:' / 'Complete tutorial error:') to preserve log greppability.

title: Small vestigial cleanups: unused isGoogleConfigured, both-branches-identical ternary in skillManager, unmounted cookieSecurity middleware
description: Three independent one-liners: (1) auth.ts:242-244 isGoogleConfigured() is defined but never called (routes call getGoogleOAuthConfig() directly) — delete. (2) skillManager.ts:84-91: `const ownerId = skill.ownerId === undefined ? (isBuiltin ? null : null) : skill.ownerId;` — the inner ternary returns null on both branches, so isBuiltin is consulted for nothing; the whole thing is `skill.ownerId ?? null` (and the preceding comment about built-ins being 'system-owned' no longer matches what the code does). (3) cookieSecurity.ts:61-76 exports a cookieSecurity() middleware whose docstring says it 'enforces secure flags on any Set-Cookie header' — it has a full test suite (services/__tests__/cookieSecurity.test.ts) but is never mounted in index.ts, so it currently protects nothing. Owner decision needed: mount it (one app.use line, mild behavior change) or delete middleware+tests; as written the docstring is misleading.
sketch: (1) Delete auth.ts:242-244. (2) In normalizeSkill: `const ownerId = skill.ownerId ?? null; const shared = skill.shared === undefined ? skill.builtin === true : !!skill.shared;` and drop the isBuiltin temp/stale comment. (3) Either add `app.use(cookieSecurity());` near the security-headers middleware in index.ts (~line 128) or remove cookieSecurity.ts + its test file (~170 lines).
impact: ~15-170 lines removed depending on the cookieSecurity decision; removes a misleading 'protection in place' impression
guidance: Two refinements. (a) skillManager.ts:82-83 comment is only half stale: 'globally shared by default' still accurately describes the shared computation at line 88-90 — reword the comment (e.g. 'Built-in plugins are globally shared by default; ownerId defaults to null') rather than deleting it, so the still-true rationale for the shared default isn't lost. (b) cookieSecurity: do NOT silently choose the mount option — mounting wraps res.setHeader on every request and is a (mild) behavior change, even though no cookies are issued today; only deletion or docstring-fix is strictly behavior-preserving. If the owner opts to keep-but-not-mount, at minimum amend the cookieSecurity.ts:3-19 docstring to state it is not currently mounted. Deleting removes cookieSecurity.ts (77 lines) + services/__tests__/cookieSecurity.test.ts (11 tests), which lowers the api test count from the 244 baseline — expected, not a regression.
